steps.title: Distribute the negative sign
steps.content:
To expand $(5x^3 - 3) - (-4x^3 + 8)$, distribute the subtraction across the second parentheses. Each term inside changes sign:
$$5x^3 - 3 - (-4x^3) - 8$$
Which becomes:
$$5x^3 - 3 + 4x^3 - 8$$

steps.title: Combine like terms to find b
steps.content:
Collect the $x^3$ terms together and the constants together:
$$(5x^3 + 4x^3) + (-3 - 8) = 9x^3 - 11$$
The simplified expression matches the form $bx^3 - 11$, so $b = 9$.
